invariants.description: The arcade must be a self-contained Local Arcade with three classic games (Snake, 2048, Minesweeper) that runs offline via a single entry point on a modern desktop machine.

do: Include exactly three games accessible from a single entry point, fully functional offline
dont: Require network connectivity, installation of runtimes, or separate files for each game

invariants.commentary: The mission defines the core deliverable: a self-contained, offline arcade.

invariants.description: The arcade must be a delightful, durable product demonstrating autonomous end-to-end engineering from design through implementation to documentation.

do: Create a polished, enjoyable experience; handle the entire lifecycle autonomously
dont: Ship a prototype lacking polish or request external guidance for implementation

invariants.commentary: This evaluates autonomous engineering capability from requirements to working product.

invariants.description: The arcade must prioritize player-centric fun: clarity, responsiveness, and discoverability take precedence over clever implementation.

do: Design immediately understandable interfaces; ensure games respond quickly to input
dont: Sacrifice clarity for elegant code patterns or hide important information

invariants.commentary: Technical excellence should serve the player experience, not overshadow it.

invariants.description: A non-technical user must be able to receive the arcade, open a single obvious entry point, and start playing within seconds without installing tools.

do: Provide one main file that opens directly in a standard modern desktop browser
dont: Require command-line commands, build steps, or specialized software

invariants.commentary: Single-artifact simplicity ensures the launch experience is as simple as opening a document.

invariants.description: The arcade must ship with human-readable documentation explaining what was built, how to run it, and how each game works.

do: Provide overview doc describing purpose, games, and basic rules for unfamiliar players
dont: Ship only source code with no descriptive documentation

invariants.commentary: Documentation transforms code into a comprehensible product.

invariants.description: The arcade must handle corrupted or missing saved data gracefully, never trapping users in broken states.

do: Reset to safe defaults when stored data is unusable; provide clear recovery options
dont: Crash or hang when stored data is missing or malformed

invariants.commentary: Real-world environments are messy. Prioritize preserving playability.

invariants.description: The arcade must ship exactly three games: Snake (continuous movement, growing snake, collision end), 2048 (sliding tiles, merging, grid fills), Minesweeper (hidden mines, numerical hints, logic reveal).

do: Implement all three games recognizably faithful to classic gameplay with win/loss conditions
dont: Replace a game with a variant that changes core mechanics or ship incomplete prototypes

invariants.commentary: These specific games provide a clear benchmark for correctness and user expectations.

invariants.description: The arcade must provide a single clear home screen acting as the hub for all three games.

do: Present home screen with game names, obvious actions to start each, and way to return
dont: Require opening separate files or force full restart to switch games

invariants.commentary: The arcade should feel like one cohesive product with a central hub.

invariants.description: The arcade must be fully functional offline without any reliance on network connectivity.

do: Allow all games to be played in airplane mode; bundle all required assets
dont: Fetch game logic or data from remote servers at runtime

invariants.description: The arcade must persist each game's best results and basic play history locally, surviving closure.

do: Remember best scores across sessions; handle absence of prior data gracefully
dont: Forget all scores when closed or rely on cloud storage

invariants.commentary: Persisted scores make the arcade feel like a product the player can grow with.

invariants.description: Every game must clearly communicate its objective, controls, and current status without requiring external documentation.

do: Display controls, current score, and clearly define win/loss states within the game
dont: Expect players to know controls from prior knowledge or require reading README

invariants.commentary: A human player should understand the experience through the interface itself.

invariants.description: The arcade must feature beautiful, polished UI with modern styling, smooth animations, and delightful interactions.

do: Use clean visual design with thoughtful colors; implement smooth animations
dont: Ship bare-bones interface with default browser styling or clashing colors

invariants.commentary: Visual design quality is a key indicator of product maturity.

invariants.description: Snake: continuously moving snake, grows when eating items, ends on collision with wall/self, allows quick restart.

do: Move snake continuously over time; end game on collision with clear game-over display
dont: Turn Snake into turn-based puzzle or ignore collisions

invariants.commentary: Snake should feel like a classic reflex game: simple, responsive, immediately understandable.

invariants.description: 2048: fixed-size grid, tiles slide and merge on equal value, new tiles appear after moves, ends when no moves remain.

do: Merge equal tiles on collision; end game when no valid moves with clear indication
dont: Allow inconsistent merging or continue indefinitely when blocked

invariants.commentary: Faithfulness to 2048's core rules is essential to be recognizable and satisfying.

invariants.description: Minesweeper: hidden mines, reveal/mark actions, numerical hints for adjacent mines, cascade reveal, clear win/loss states.

do: Show numbers indicating adjacent mines; cascade reveal on empty cells; clear loss on mine reveal
dont: Omit numerical hints or leave win condition ambiguous

invariants.commentary: Correct win/loss logic and numerical hints are central to the Minesweeper experience.

invariants.description: The system must independently derive technical design, implement, and verify correctness without low-level implementation instructions.

do: Translate high-level requirements into architecture; design and run own tests
dont: Request external guidance for file layouts, frameworks, or code-level decisions

invariants.commentary: This evaluates the system's ability to go from requirements to working, tested implementation.
